name: Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python
summary: Python API for Bluefruit LE Sniffer

documentation.readme: This repository contains the Python API for Adafruit's Bluefruit LE Sniffer, and our easy to use API wrapper.

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: Target device is using random address

arguments.name: --random_txaddr
arguments.description: Target device is using random address

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: verbose mode (all serial traffic is displayed)

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: verbose mode (all serial traffic is displayed)

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with pybluez/pybluez (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with airgproducts/pybluez2 (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_BLE (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: Python API for Bluefruit LE Sniffer.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: This repository contains the Python API for Adafruit's Bluefruit LE Sniffer, and our easy to use API wrapper.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python sniffer.py /dev/tty.usbmodem1412311`. Further invocations from upstream: `Logging data to logs/capture.pcap`; `Connecting to sniffer on /dev/tty.usbmodem1412311`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python because Python API for Bluefruit LE Sniffer.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python sniffer.py /dev/tty.usbmodem1412311` as documented upstream for Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Logging data to logs/capture.pcap` as documented upstream for Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Connecting to sniffer on /dev/tty.usbmodem1412311` as documented upstream for Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Scanning for BLE devices (5s) ...` as documented upstream for Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Adafruit_BLESniffer_Python --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
